releases.title: collectZ v3.16.23
releases.summary: This patch continues CodeQL baseline remediation by hardening log output against forged-line injection and documenting the intent-first process for unused-variable findings before broader cleanup.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Sanitized request, audit, and syslog collector log fields before writing untrusted values to console or log files.
Preserved Plex webhook and token redaction while also collapsing control characters in request URLs.
Added unit coverage for shared log-field sanitization.
Wired Delicious platform canonicalization through the sorted platform alias key list.
Added unit coverage for the longer `Xbox Series X` platform suffix so the alias ordering intent remains protected.
Documented the unused-variable triage rule in the backlog and selected roadmap slice.

releases.title: collectZ v3.16.22
releases.summary: This patch continues CodeQL baseline remediation by clearing local SQL-injection and incomplete URL substring-host findings while preserving the existing media identity matching and provider-link labeling behavior.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Parameterized media identity alias SQL comparisons for ISBN, UPC/EAN, ASIN, provider item, Metron issue, and Plex alias matching.
Removed escaped alias-key interpolation from the affected media provider matching queries.
Added unit coverage that keeps media identity alias SQL on query parameters.
Replaced legacy AudioDB URL substring detection with parsed-hostname matching.
Replaced broad source-link hostname substring checks in the Library detail drawer with parsed hostname label matching.
Updated the PriceCharting unit-test mock to compare parsed hostnames instead of broad URL substrings.

releases.title: collectZ v3.16.21
releases.summary: This patch starts the CodeQL baseline remediation work by hardening backend input boundaries for user-supplied calendar feeds, Kavita connector URLs, and CSV import upload handling.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Added a shared outbound URL policy for user-supplied ICS feeds.
Blocked non-HTTP(S), credential-bearing, localhost, private, loopback, and link-local ICS feed targets by default.
Added `ALLOW_PRIVATE_ICS_FEEDS=true` for deliberate private-feed deployments or controlled local test topologies.
Tightened Kavita base URL normalization to HTTP(S) URLs without embedded credentials while preserving LAN-hosted Kavita support.
Moved generic, Calibre, and Delicious CSV imports to multer memory buffers instead of request-controlled temp file paths.
Added unit coverage for the URL policy, Kavita normalization, and in-memory CSV import boundary.

releases.title: collectZ v3.16.20
releases.summary: This patch keeps the collapsed sidebar brand mark aligned with the navigation icon column. It continues the 3.16 sidebar cleanup without changing routes, permissions, or the existing hamburger collapse behavior.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Centered the collapsed sidebar brand header content in the narrow rail.
Kept the collectZ mark visually in line with the collapsed navigation icons.
Kept collapsed icon-only nav buttons accessible by preserving their text labels as `aria-label` values.
Preserved the expanded sidebar header behavior from `3.16.19`.
Left the hamburger/collapse affordance unchanged while the top-brand-as-collapse-control idea remains a product follow-up.

releases.title: collectZ v3.16.19
releases.summary: This patch simplifies the sidebar brand header by aligning the collectZ mark with the navigation icon column and removing the version badge from the top chrome.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Shifted the expanded sidebar collectZ mark right so it lines up vertically with navigation icons.
Removed the visible version number from the sidebar header.
Kept Help > Releases as the canonical in-app place to read the current build and release history.
Updated browser coverage so the nav header no longer expects the version badge.

releases.title: collectZ v3.16.18
releases.summary: This patch tightens the sidebar underline interaction so selected items use the same bright blue as tab selection, while account menu rows use the same quieter hover treatment as the main navigation.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Fixed selected sidebar underlines so the active state uses the bright app blue instead of inheriting the muted hover color.
Kept inactive sidebar hover/focus underlines muted.
Removed the distracting active/focus outline from the account trigger when the profile menu is open.
Updated profile menu rows to use text hover plus muted underline feedback instead of gray block hover fills.
Kept the active profile row on a solid blue underline when the profile page is selected.

releases.title: collectZ v3.16.17
releases.summary: This patch refines sidebar navigation feedback so hover and active states use compact blue underlines instead of gray row highlighting.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Removed muted gray hover backgrounds from sidebar navigation items.
Added a muted blue hover/focus underline that appears only under the hovered or focused item.
Shortened sidebar underlines so they read closer to the label width instead of spanning the full nav row.
Kept active selected items on a solid blue underline.
Preserved the collapsed-rail active indicator for grouped Library and Platform navigation.
Updated unit coverage for the hover underline contract.

releases.title: collectZ v3.16.16
releases.summary: This patch replaces the heavy sidebar active highlight with a quieter blue underline that matches the app's tab selection language.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Replaced the large raised active block on sidebar nav items with a compact blue underline.
Kept selected nav text readable without changing routes or permissions.
Kept Library and Platform parent rows calm while visible child rows own the selected underline.
Preserved a collapsed-rail indicator for grouped Library and Platform navigation.
Updated browser and unit coverage for the new sidebar active-state contract.

releases.title: collectZ v3.16.15
releases.summary: This patch cleans up the Workspace sidebar by keeping primary library categories together and moving Loans, Review, and Wishlist into a separated Library utility group.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Moved `Loans` below the primary library category list.
Renamed the visible `Merge Review` sidebar entry to `Review`.
Moved `Review` into the Library section instead of showing it as a separate Workspace item.
Kept the existing Merge Review route and page behavior.
Kept `Wishlist` in the separated Library utility group.
Kept `Import`, `Help`, and `Settings` as direct Workspace navigation items.
Updated browser and unit coverage for the new navigation structure.

releases.title: collectZ v3.16.14
releases.summary: This patch treats Capture Inbox as an intake workflow instead of a Library category by moving its entry point under Import.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Removed `Capture Inbox` from the Library navigation list.
Kept `Wishlist` in Library.
Added `Capture Inbox` as a first-class tab on the Import surface.
Kept existing Capture Inbox routes and deep links working.
Made the `Import` navigation item stay active when Capture Inbox is open.
Updated browser and unit coverage for the new Import-owned Capture entry point.
